mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.0% upward.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, forcing equity markets to price in immediate earnings downgrades and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a rank IC greater than 0.02 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation, rising consumer prices erode real corporate margins and force central banks to tighten policy, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL accelerates above the 0.3% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and rising corporate revenues, which mechanically lift equity valuations and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls additions drop below 100,000, weak labor demand signals economic deceleration that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100.0 thousand
failure_condition: Markets rally in 75% of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below the 100,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising consumer prices creates stagflationary conditions that compress equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-CPIAUCSL divergence fails to generate a statistically significant negative return differential (p > 0.10) over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary environment, consecutive monthly declines in industrial production confirm supply-side contraction that outweighs monetary support, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in 80% of cases where INDPRO falls for two straight months during tightening periods.
